rulings.Case Title: LVNV FUNDING LLC VS. SALLY WIATROLIK et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Ntc Of Hearing On Claim Of Exemption; Ntc Of Oppos
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Tuesday, April 30, 2013, Line 2, PLAINTIFF LVNV FUNDING LLC'S Ntc Of Hearing On Claim Of Exemption; Ntc Of Oppos Hearing required. Granted in part and denied in part. Judgment debtor failed to show that any of the funds can be traced to worker's compensation payments deposited into her account. Judgment debtor should bring to the hearing documents to establish that the checking account contains worker's compensation payment. However, judgment debtor has shown that $2,485 of the funds levied upon are paid earnings. Paid earnings are 75% exempt under Code of Civ. Pro. section 704.070(b)(2). Accordingly, $1,863.75 shall be released to the judgment debtor. The remaining amount of $2,920.83 shall be released to the judgment creditor for payment toward the judgment. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. =(302/MJM)

rulings.Case Title: CARLOS BONILLA, AS AN INDIVIDUAL AND ON BEHALF OF VS. PRESSE, LLC DBA CAFE DE LA PRESSE et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Joint Motion For 1) Preliminary Approval Of Class Action Settlement; 2) Prelim Certification Of Settlement Class; And 3) Approval Of Noice Of Class Action Lawsuit
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Tuesday, April 30, 2013, Line 9, DEFENDANT PRESSE, LLC DBA CAFE DE LA PRESSE'S Joint Motion For 1) Preliminary Approval Of Class Action Settlement; 2) Prelim Certification Of Settlement Class; And 3) Approval Of Noice Of Class Action Lawsuit Hearing required. Counsel are to be prepared to address and explain: 1. Attorneys fees and costs in Joint Motion are "not to exceed $70,500 (30% of the Settlement Proceeds)." (Motion at 6.) However, Settlement Agreement states "Class Counsel will apply to the Court for an award of Class Counsel's Fees and Court Approved Costs, the fees portion of which will not exceed $70,500 of the Settlement Fund (30%) before Class Counsel's approved expenses are deducted..." (Set. Agreement at 13:5-8) (emphasis added). 2. What are estimated costs, including settlement administrator's costs, and how and when is the sum paid? From the $70,500? Notice, page 7, item 2 states that $70,500 includes attorney fees and costs. 3. Class period is defined as "during the period commencing 4 years from the filing of this action through the entry of final judgment of this action." (Set. Agreement para IV A.) However, Class notice is addressed to those employed from June 6, 2007 "to [date of preliminary approval]" of settlement, and Notice consistently refers to this as the class period throughout. See, e.g., Notice, item 1 ("What is this lawsuit about?"), first sentence; item 2 ("Why did I receive this Notice?"). "Entry of final judgment" date isn't consistent with opportunity to file a claim, since notice won't be given to employees who are hired later than 10 days after preliminary approval but before entry of final judgment. (Set. Agreement p.9:7-13.) 4. The Court will also address in detail the structure and substance of the proposed class notice. =(302/MJM)

rulings.Case Title: DIANE LINEHAN VS. BAXTER INTERNATIONAL, INC. et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion To Compel Access To Decedent'S Electronic Medical Record At The Depositions Of St. Mary'S Medical Center Nurses
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Tuesday, April 30, 2013, Line 1. PLAINTIFF DIANE LINEHAN'S Motion to Compel Access to Decedent's Electronic Medical Record at the Depositions of St. Mary's Medical Center Nurses Pro Tem Judge Bruce Highman, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: The motion is granted. The EMR is relevant under the broad standard of relevancy applicable in discovery, notwithstanding there having been software upgrades since plaintiff's decedent died. The 60 day time limit applies, and the motion is timely. Even if the 60 day time limit didn't apply, the court would find that there was good cause for filing the motion later than 45 days after service of the objections to the deposition notice. The court plans to sign plaintiff's proposed order at the hearing. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to bruce.highman@highman-ball.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: JASON COOK VS. MERCEDES-BENZ USA, LLC et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Mtn To Strike Or Tax Mercedez Costs
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Tuesday, April 30, 2013, Line 13, PLAINTIFF JASON COOK'S Mtn To Strike Or Tax Mercedez Costs The motion to strike costs is denied. The motion to tax costs is granted only as to $73.82 in travel costs which Mercedes-Benz USA LLC ("MB") concedes are an arithmetic error. Defendant MB is a prevailing party under CCP § 1332(a)(4) because it is a "defendant as against those plaintiffs who do not recovery any relief against that defendant." The Court granted Defendant MB's contested motion for summary judgment. Judgment was entered in favor of Defendant and against Plaintiff Jason Cook. Plaintiff has failed to show that the costs were not reasonable or necessary. Costs are awarded to Defendant MB in the amount of $1841.28. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/MJM)

rulings.Case Title: LORI WILLIAMS VS. GEORGE P. SURMAITIS et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER TO 2ND AMENDED COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Tuesday, April 30, 2013, Line 19, DEFENDANT HANNA LEUNG, D/B/A LEUNG & ASSOCIATES' DEMURRER TO 2ND AMENDED COMPLAINT Per the Presiding Judge, this matter is assigned to Department 611 before Judge Taylor to be heard on April 30, 2013 at 9:00 am. The parties are required to appear. Judge Taylor has issued the following tentative ruling: Defendants' demurrer to the Second Amended Complaint is sustained without leave to amend. Where a plaintiff sues a defendant for legal malpractice alleging several distinct acts of malpractice with respect to a single representation, a demurrer is properly granted on the basis of the statute of limitations only if each alleged act of malpractice is time barred. Pointe San Diego Residential Community, L.P. v. Procopio, Cory, Hargreaves & Savitch, LLP (App. 4 Dist. 2011) 125 Cal.Rptr.3d 540, 195 Cal.App.4th 265. The one-year limitations period for a legal malpractice action is triggered when the client discovers, or should have discovered, the facts constituting the wrongful act or omission and not by his/her discovery that such facts constitute professional negligence, i.e., by discovery that a particular legal theory is applicable based on the known facts. Truong v. Glasser (App. 4 Dist. 2009) 103 Cal.Rptr.3d 811, 181 Cal.App.4th 102. The gravamen of the Second Amended Complaint is one for legal malpractice. So, the one year statute of limitations applies. But what about the Second Cause of Action for which is labeled "Fraud" and the Third Cause of Action for "Intentional Tort? When read with all of the other allegations and causes of action, the fraud and intentional tort allegations are really alleging a breach of fiduciary duty and, thus, are subject to the one year statute of limitations as well. Stoll v Superior Court (1992) 9 Cal. App. 4th 1362; Quintilliani v Mannerino (1998) 62 Cal. App. 4th 54, 67. The issues presented by this demurrer are: 1. When did Plaintiff, Lori Williams, discover or when should he have discovered that she had been harmed within Laird v. Blacker, supra, 2 Cal.4th 606, and ITT Small Business Finance Corp. v. Niles, supra, 9 Cal.4th 245? 2. Is the negligence cause of action alleged barred by the statute of limitations? 3. Are all the causes of action barred by the statute of limitations? Issue 1: What does the complaint say about when Lori Williams discovered or should have discovered that she was harmed? The Second Amended Complaint alleges that in March 2011, Lori Williams, thought she was going to be represented by Hanna Leung at the Worker's Comp. Hearing, but George P. Surmaitis showed up, tried to coerce her to settle the case, and failed to advise her of her rights to appeal. So, in March of 2011, Lori Williams discovered or should have discovered of the alleged malpractice. Issue 2: When did the one year statute of limitations run? The statute ran in March of 2012. The original complaint, filed July 23, 2012, was filed more than one year after the March 2011 Workers Comp. Hearing. Thus, on the date the original complaint was filed, the statute of limitations had run on bringing the legal malpractice complaint. Issue 3: The court finds that although the 2nd and 3rd causes of action are labeled "Fraud" and "Intentional Tort", essentially they are complaints alleging malpractice and breach of trust. The complaints are about what was said and how Plaintiff was represented at the Workers' Comp. Hearing. Therefore they are also barred by the one year statute of limitations. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/MJM)

rulings.Case Title: KIRK KELLEY-KHAN VS. HOTEL MONACO et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Kirk Kelley-Khan'S Notice Of Motion And Motion To Quash Or Limit Subpoena Seeking Medical Records And Reimbursement Of Reasonable Expenses From Defts
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Tuesday, April 30, 2013, Line 5. Plaintiff Kirk Kelley-Khan's Motion to Quash or Limit Subpoena Seeking Medical Records and Reimbursement of Reasonable Expenses from Defendants Pro Tem Judge Bruce Highman, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Hearing required. Sanctions are denied. Plaintiff shall bring the subpoenas at issue to the hearing. The remainder of this tentative assumes the only subpoena sought to be quashed is for Dr. Johnson's records. Plaintiff's interrogatory answers list specific medical conditions allegedly caused or exacerbated by defendants, and additionally states defendants caused other, unspecified bodily aches and pains. If plaintiff continues to say at the hearing that his injuries include other, unspecified bodily aches and pains, then all of Dr. Johnson's records from April 5, 2008, through the present, other than radiological films, x-rays, MRIs, CT scans, and dental histories shall be produced. If plaintiff agrees at the hearing to limit his claim to only the specific medical conditions listed in the interrogatory answers and deposition, then only medical records from April 5, 2008, through the present relating to nausea, headaches, insomnia, stress, anxiety, emotional distress, and depression, and plaintiff's back and knee will be produced to defendants. Pages of the medical records that relate only to other conditions will not be produced to defendants. Where part of a page relates to one of the specified conditions, and part relates to other conditions, the part of the page that relates to other conditions may be redacted before it is produced to defendants. The copy service shall copy and Bates stamp all records from April 5, 2008, through the present, and mail a copy to plaintiff's attorney Jody Meisel. Within ten calendar days after she receives the records, she shall serve defendants and the copy service a declaration under penalty of perjury stating by Bates number all the pages that she is withholding wholly or partly because they relate to other medical conditions. She shall also serve defendants a copy of all pages where nothing is being withheld. For each page where she redacts a part thereof because it relates to other medical conditions, she shall serve a copy on defendants and the copy service of the page as redacted. Within ten days after they receive Ms. Meisel's designations, defendants shall inform Bruce Highman which Bates numbered pages, if any, they contend may have been improperly withheld wholly or partly. Ms. Meisel shall then serve as soon as possible the unredacted copies of all the allegedly improperly withheld Bates pages on Bruce Highman who shall decide which, if any, were improperly withheld. Each party is required to bring a proposed order to the hearing. =(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: MITCHELL ENGINEERING COMPANY VS. CMC TRAFFIC CONTROL SPECIALISTS INC., et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Tuesday, April 30, 2013, Line 29, DEFENDANT CMC TRAFFIC CONTROL SPECIALISTS INC.'S, DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT Overruled. Mitchell Engineering Company has pled sufficient facts to allege the causes of action for breach of contract and quantum meruit. The causes of action do not appear time-barred on the face of the Complaint. It cannot be determined at the pleading stage whether the Statute of Frauds applies. Both Mitchell Engineering Company and CMC Traffic Control Specialists, Inc. include additional facts in their moving and opposing papers that are outside of the allegations of the Complaint and not considered by the court. CMC Traffic's objections to the Jones Declaration are sustained. Defendant's request for judicial notice of the complaint is granted. The request for judicial notice of the Petition for Dissolution is denied; it is not referred to in the Complaint and is irrelevant. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/MJM)
